title: cymbachne guineensis subvar. gayana
intro: Cymbachne guineensis subvar. gayana, also known as the Guinea love grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to West Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihelmintic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, malaria, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of diabetes and hypertension. The herbal preparations of Cymbachne guineensis subvar. gayana include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
